name: OWASP Top 10 Web Application Security Risks
description: The most critical web application security risks, mapped to Azure mitigations. Summaries are paraphrased.

controls.title: Broken Access Control
controls.summary: Users can act outside their intended permissions due to missing or flawed authorization checks.
controls.remediation: Enforce server-side authorization on every route, deny by default, use Entra ID app roles, and test with automated authz suites.

controls.title: Cryptographic Failures
controls.summary: Sensitive data is exposed through weak or missing encryption in transit or at rest.
controls.remediation: TLS 1.2+ everywhere, HSTS, platform encryption at rest, secrets in Key Vault, no custom crypto.

controls.title: Injection
controls.summary: Untrusted data alters queries or commands (SQLi, XSS, command injection).
controls.remediation: Parameterized queries/ORM, output encoding, strict input validation, WAF managed rules as defense in depth.

controls.title: Insecure Design
controls.summary: Missing security requirements and threat modeling produce architecturally unsafe systems.
controls.remediation: Threat model (STRIDE) during design, define abuse cases, and adopt secure design patterns and reference architectures.

controls.title: Security Misconfiguration
controls.summary: Insecure defaults, open cloud storage, verbose errors, or missing hardening expose the system.
controls.remediation: Harden via IaC baselines, Azure Policy enforcement, disable unused features, and scan configs in CI.

controls.title: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
controls.summary: Known-vulnerable dependencies and images are exploitable.
controls.remediation: SCA and container scanning in CI (Trivy), patch SLAs, and Defender vulnerability management in runtime.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication Failures
controls.summary: Weak authentication enables credential stuffing, session hijacking, and account takeover.
controls.remediation: Delegate auth to Entra ID (OIDC), enforce MFA, secure session handling, and risk-based sign-in policies.

controls.title: Software and Data Integrity Failures
controls.summary: Unverified updates, CI/CD tampering, or insecure deserialization compromise integrity.
controls.remediation: Signed artifacts, protected branches, OIDC-based deploys (no long-lived secrets), and integrity checks on pipelines.

controls.title: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
controls.summary: Insufficient logging delays breach detection and response.
controls.remediation: Structured security logging to Log Analytics, alerting on auth anomalies, and tested incident escalation paths.

controls.title: Server-Side Request Forgery
controls.summary: The server is tricked into making requests to unintended destinations, often internal services or metadata endpoints.
controls.remediation: Allow-list outbound destinations, block link-local metadata addresses, validate and normalize URLs, and segment egress via Azure Firewall.
